hero.description: I’m a software engineer with a degree in programming. In my day-to-day work, I develop Building Management Systems (BMS) using .NET, ASP.NET , C#, Blazor, DevExpress, PostgreSQL, and JavaScript. Outside of work, I build modern web applications with Next.js, React, TypeScript, and Node.js.

budgetapp.label: Personal finance
budgetapp.title: Financial App
budgetapp.description: A personal finance tracker built with Next.js that lets users track expenses and income, import transactions from ING CSV files, and review everything in a dashboard.

catfacts.label: API and frontend
catfacts.title: Cat & Facts
catfacts.description: A responsive app fetching cat images and facts from a custom API, focused on simplicity and smooth interactions.

lurniflow.label: Company website
lurniflow.title: LurniFlow
lurniflow.description: An app that turns what the user learns during a reading session into flashcards, helping reinforce the most important information in a more active way. The project is self-hosted and deployed without Vercel using GitHub Actions.

sections.title: Project overview
sections.content: A modern BMS/SCADA application for monitoring building installations in real time. The system makes it possible to visualize device data, analyze historical trends, track alarms and events, and present the current state of a facility through dynamic browser-based views.

sections.title: My role
sections.content: I was responsible for developing key frontend elements in Blazor and integrating them with the .NET backend. I built components for device visualization, status and event handling, user permission logic, and advanced Highcharts charts with zooming, data export, tooltips, event flags, and automatic refresh.

sections.title: My achievements
sections.content: The most important things I delivered:
items: I optimized Highcharts chart performance, reducing render time by 50% for large historical data ranges.
I refactored chart data generation logic into separate classes, increasing unit test coverage from 60% to 85%.
I created over 30 unit tests for Blazor components, improving stability and making future application development easier.

qrReader.label: Backend service
qrReader.title: QR Code Reader Service
qrReader.description: A Python service for reading QR codes from provided images, designed for integration with larger workflows and automated data processing.

photoExif.label: Backend service
photoExif.title: Photo EXIF Reader Service
photoExif.description: A Python service for extracting EXIF metadata from photos, used to read technical image details and prepare them for downstream processing.

contact.heading: Get in touch
contact.description: I’m always open to discussing potential collaborations, new projects, or just exchanging ideas. Feel free to reach out, and I’ll get back to you as soon as possible!
